plan.goal: Address all 5 code review findings from Phase 2 review
plan.context: Phase 2 code review found 5 issues across wflib/record.py, wflib/worktree.py, and tests/integration/. All are small, independent fixes. The codebase has 288 passing tests and 133 skipped (Phase 3+). No fix should break existing tests.

tasks.title: Fix record.py: use _now_iso() and strengthen record_event signature
tasks.goal: Address findings 4 and 5. (1) Replace the inline timestamp formatting in create_record with a call to _now_iso(). (2) Change record_event's event parameter from str to ImplementationEventType so invalid values are caught at the call site, not inside a cast. If any callers in tests pass raw strings, update them to use the enum.
constraints: create_record must use _now_iso() for created_at instead of duplicating the datetime formatting
record_event signature should accept ImplementationEventType directly, not str — remove the ImplementationEventType(event) cast inside the body
Update the docstring to reflect the new type
Fix any test call sites that pass raw strings to record_event
acceptance: python3 -m pytest tests/ --tb=short passes with same counts (288 passed, 133 skipped)
grep confirms no duplicate datetime formatting in create_record

tasks.title: Fix worktree.py: no-commits fallback and document mid-rebase intent
tasks.goal: Address findings 2 and 3. (1) In commit_or_amend_workflow_files, handle the case where git log -1 fails (brand-new repo with no commits) by falling back to a fresh commit instead of raising RuntimeError. (2) In merge_back, add a clear code comment explaining that the rebase is intentionally left in-progress on conflict so the caller (task_executor) can spawn a resolution agent.
constraints: commit_or_amend_workflow_files: if git log -1 fails (no commits), treat as 'no previous workflow commit' and do a fresh commit
merge_back: add a comment block above the early return explaining the intentional mid-rebase state — reference task_executor._merge_and_cleanup as the consumer
Do NOT change merge_back behavior — only add documentation
acceptance: python3 -m pytest tests/ --tb=short passes with same counts (288 passed, 133 skipped)
commit_or_amend_workflow_files works on a repo with no prior commits (add a test case)

tasks.title: Extract shared init_repo helper for integration tests
tasks.goal: Address finding 1. Extract the duplicated init_repo helper from test_git.py and test_worktree.py into a shared module at tests/integration/helpers.py. Update both test files to import from the shared module. The shared version should support the with_commit parameter (from test_git.py's version).
constraints: Create tests/integration/helpers.py with the shared init_repo function
The shared init_repo must support with_commit=True (default) parameter from test_git.py's version
Remove the local init_repo definitions from both test files and replace with imports from helpers
Do not change any test logic — only the helper location
acceptance: python3 -m pytest tests/ --tb=short passes with same counts (288 passed, 133 skipped)
grep -rn 'def init_repo' tests/integration/ shows only one definition in helpers.py

task-1.title: Fix record.py: use _now_iso() and strengthen record_event signature
task-1.summary: Fixed two code review findings in `wflib/record.py`: (1) replaced inline datetime formatting in `create_record` with a call to `_now_iso()`, and (2) changed `record_event`'s `event` parameter type from `str` to `ImplementationEventType`, removing the internal cast and updating the docstring and test call sites.
task-1.diffStat:
tests/integration/test_record.py |  4 ++--
 wflib/record.py                  | 15 +++++++++++----
 2 files changed, 13 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)

task-3.title: Extract shared init_repo helper for integration tests
task-3.summary: Extracted the duplicated `init_repo` helper from `test_git.py` and `test_worktree.py` into a shared `tests/integration/helpers.py` module, with the `with_commit=True` parameter from `test_git.py`'s version. Updated both test files to import from the shared module.

task-2.title: Fix worktree.py: no-commits fallback and document mid-rebase intent
task-2.summary: Fixed two issues in `wflib/worktree.py`: (1) `commit_or_amend_workflow_files` now handles brand-new repos with no commits by falling back to a fresh commit instead of raising RuntimeError, and (2) added a comment block in `merge_back` explaining the intentional mid-rebase state on conflict for `task_executor._merge_and_cleanup`.
